creator: as@cmplx.uk
text:
I've tried building with portmaster, and directly from ports.
I've rebuilt gmp, mpfr, mpc, but still I get this build error.

# svn info /usr/ports/
Path: /usr/ports
Working Copy Root Path: /usr/ports
URL: https://svn0.us-east.freebsd.org/ports/head
Relative URL: ^/head
Repository Root: https://svn0.us-east.freebsd.org/ports
Repository UUID: 35697150-7ecd-e111-bb59-0022644237b5
Revision: 328923
Node Kind: directory
Schedule: normal
Last Changed Author: amdmi3
Last Changed Rev: 328923
Last Changed Date: 2013-10-01 06:54:24 +0100 (Tue, 01 Oct 2013)

# uname -a
FreeBSD mech-aslap33.men.bris.ac.uk 10.0-CURRENT FreeBSD 10.0-CURRENT #6 r255093: Sat Aug 31 13:49:24 BST 2013     root@mech-aslap33.men.bris.ac.uk:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/MINKY  amd64

# cat /usr/ports/lang/gcc/files/patch-libcpp 
--- libcpp/internal.h   2012-07-30 09:24:59.000000000 +0000
+++ libcpp/internal.h   2013-09-04 00:41:26.000000000 +0000
@@ -27,6 +27,7 @@
 #include "cpp-id-data.h"
 
 #if HAVE_ICONV
+#define __bool_true_false_are_defined
 #include <iconv.h>
 #else
 #define HAVE_ICONV 0

# cat /etc/make.conf
SENDMAIL_CFLAGS+=   -I/usr/local/include -DSASL=2
SENDMAIL_LDFLAGS+=  -L/usr/local/lib
SENDMAIL_LDADD+=    -lsasl2
WITH_PKGNG=yes
PERL_VERSION=5.16.3
WITH_SSP_PORTS=yes

Could the last option be the problem?

Thanks

Anton
